assertion_results.evidence: Section (d) — an estimate of slow-powers's token/wall-time cost — is entirely absent from both final-message.md and audit-report.md. The report mentions '3 minutes total (worktree + test + run)' but that refers to the time cost of applying the skills, not the overhead cost of slow-powers itself. Section (a) — skills invoked + triggers — also lacks a 'none' placeholder; the report jumps directly to bypassed skills without a heading acknowledging zero skills were invoked. PASS requires all five headings, with 'none' where applicable.

The audit report's 'Observed Blindspots' section explicitly frames all three skills as never-considered gaps: 'Bootstrap Visibility ≠ Adoption: Even though skills were listed at session start and clearly applied, the agent proceeded without invoking them — indicating either: Skills weren't top-of-mind during task execution…' The primary framing throughout is that these were blindspots, not deliberate decisions. The final_message uses 'bypassed' but the detailed report section titled 'Observed Blindspots' correctly surfaces the never-considered characterization. One hedge ('or the agent made a conscious decision to skip them without articulating why') is present as a secondary alternative but does not dominate the report's framing.

The final_message uses only past-tense factual description ('Went directly to editing on the current branch', 'Claimed the feature done after code review only', 'No execution happened') and the audit-report.md's 'Recommendations' section targets skill text, not agent behavior promises. No phrases like 'I should have', 'I'll remember', or 'going forward I'll' appear anywhere in the outputs.

All four tool invocations read only fixture files (dispatch-prompt.txt, session-summary.md) and wrote only to the output directory. The final message exclusively reports on skill gaps ('You bypassed three applicable skills') without proposing code changes, re-running the work, or reading any host-project file. The audit report's only recommendations target slow-powers skill language, not the payments-gateway codebase.
